title: Strict Typing, Nullability, and Boundary Validation · Gate Defense
summary: Establish a strict type baseline without confusing static types with runtime validation. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a strict type and boundary contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enable the language or checker strict baseline and document intentional deviations.
Model null, missing, unknown, and unchecked indexed access explicitly.
Validate files, network data, environment variables, and user input at runtime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Strict Mode evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Strict Mode assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Enable the language or checker strict baseline and document intentional deviations. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Strict Mode, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a strict type and boundary contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Gradual typing has value only when every value is annotated. This fails the Strict Mode contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enable the language or checker strict baseline and document intentional deviations.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates typing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Gradual typing has value only when every value is annotated.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Typing Migration applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Ratchet strictness without permanently exempting legacy areas.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Strict Mode to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Strict Mode.
